items.text:
Dining Hall Allergen Policy Update

The University Dining Services will implement a new allergen labeling policy beginning Monday, September 11. The policy affects all four main dining halls and applies to every meal period from 7:00 AM to 9:00 PM daily.

Key changes:
- All hot-line dishes will be labeled with the top nine allergens (milk, eggs, fish, shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soy, and sesame).
- A dedicated allergen-free preparation station will operate in Sutter Hall.
- Students with a confirmed dietary restriction may receive a $5 weekly meal credit by signing up at the Health Services office.

To enroll, please contact Ms. Quan in the Health Services office, Room 215 of the Wellness Center, between 9:00 AM and 4:00 PM weekdays. Pre-registration ensures that ingredient cards are printed before each meal period. Students are reminded to check signage at every station before serving themselves.

For full guidelines, visit the dining portal.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what must students do to receive the meal credit?

options.A: Speak with the dining manager during meals
options.B: Sign up at the Health Services office
options.C: Email Ms. Quan a list of allergens
options.D: Visit the allergen-free station each day

questions.explanation: The notice states that students with a confirmed dietary restriction may receive a $5 weekly meal credit by signing up at the Health Services office.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To list the menu items served during the fall semester
options.B: To inform students about a new allergen labeling policy
options.C: To announce the opening of a new dining hall
options.D: To explain why dining halls will close on Mondays

questions.explanation: The notice introduces and details a new allergen labeling policy that will begin in September, so its main purpose is to inform students about that policy.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students with food allergies?

options.A: They will have access to safer meal options than before.
options.B: They will need to provide a list of every ingredient.
options.C: They will be required to follow a strict dining schedule.
options.D: They will need to pay a fee for special menus.

questions.explanation: Because the new policy introduces allergen labeling, a dedicated allergen-free station, and a meal credit, students with food allergies will have access to safer options than they did before.
